free_text: Photonuclear reactions involving the emission of
deuterons and tritons with energies below 15 MeV.
The cross sections for angle 90-deg were obtained
for the reactions (g,p),(g,d) and (g,t) on Li-6 and
Li-7 nuclei. The relative yields Y(g,d)/Y(g,p) and
Y(G,T)/Y(G,P) FOR LI-6, LI-7, B-11 AND CU NUCLEI AS A
function of EG-MAX were measured. Angular distributions
of photo-protons, deuterons, and tritons for Li-7 and
the excitation function for Li-7(g,t) are presented.
It is shown that photodeutrons are predominantly
produced in reactions in which one or several particles
are emitted in addition of deuterons. The excitation
function of the Li-7(g,t) reaction and triton
angular distribution are shown to agree with the
concept of direct dipole absorption of g-quanta
by the Li-7 nucleus as a (t-a) system.
